speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: Detroit Pistons win the first-round playoff series against the Cleveland Cavaliers.
grade_note: CLE beat DET 4-3, so the Pistons did not win the series.
grade_note_general: CLE beat DET 4-3, so the Pistons did not win the series.
argument: Physicality: Detroit is simply too physical for Cleveland, with Jared Allen and Evan Mobley visibly struggling against that contact; Size mismatch: Detroit's lineup of Duren, Ausar Thompson, and Tobias Harris creates an overwhelming size and athleticism problem for CLE; Shooting woes: Cleveland went 0-for-11 from three in Q4 and 7-for-32 overall in Game 2, making road wins very difficult; Key matchup: Dennis Jenkins has won the backup-PG matchup against Dennis Schroder in both games, helping DET close both contests.

speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: Oklahoma City Thunder win the first-round playoff series against the Los Angeles Lakers in four games (sweep).
grade_note: OKC vs LAL was a second-round series (Conference Semifinals), not a first-round series; OKC's first-round opponent was PHX.
grade_note_general: OKC beat LAL 4-0, so the Thunder did win the series even though the 'first-round' label was wrong.
argument: Series momentum: OKC leads 2-0 and Draymond would be surprised if it goes five games; A.J. Mitchell: Mitchell is shooting 50% from the field and averaging 19 points, capably filling the Jalen Williams void as he has all season; Lakers' ceiling: LeBron could theoretically steal one game but Draymond doesn't expect LAL to tie the series.

speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: New York Knicks sweep the Philadelphia 76ers 4-0 and advance to the Eastern Conference Finals.
grade_note: NYK beat PHI 4-0 (sweep) and advanced to the ECF where they beat CLE 4-0.
grade_note_general: NYK beat PHI 4-0, so the Knicks did sweep the 76ers and advance to the ECF.
argument: No comeback: Philadelphia came back from 3-1 last series but will not come back from 3-0 this time; NYK rhythm: New York is playing as hot as any team with Brunson (33 pts), Bridges (23 pts), and Hart (12 pts/11 reb) all contributing in Game 3; Embiid disruption: When Embiid plays he monopolises shot attempts, squeezing Maxey (only 12 FGA in Game 3) and others out of their offensive rhythm.
